exemplar.title: Investigation of the Impact of Internal Pressure on the Coefficient of Restitution of Basketballs during Rebound

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigation of the Impact of Internal Pressure on the Coefficient of Restitution of Basketballs during Rebound

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Graphs and data tables are well organised with error bars, best-fit equations and RÂ² values.
Uncertainties are propagated appropriately for pressure, height and CoR and discussed in the text.
Data processing (averaging, regression, kinematic derivations) is carried out accurately with sound formula use.
weaknesses: Column heading formatting is occasionally cluttered, reducing clarity.
Minor typographical errors (e.g. in uncertainty formula) and a contradictory pressure uncertainty estimate appear.
Discussion omits atmospheric/systematic uncertainty components in propagation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data Analysis meets the criteria for clarity, precision and appropriate uncertainty treatment, meriting a high band score. Small formatting issues and minor calculation/typographical slips justify a score of 4 rather than the maximum.

strengths: The research question is focused and situated within a clear and specific sporting context.
Background theory and practical motivation are well explained, aiding reader understanding.
Methodology description (diagram, apparatus list, step-by-step) largely enables reproducibility.
weaknesses: Official basketball regulation pressure ranges are not cited to justify selected values.
Details such as camera model/field of view and horizontal alignment method are omitted.
Subscript notation in equations could be clearer to avoid confusion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place Research Design solidly in the top band (5â6), but minor omissions (exact pressure range citation, precise camera and alignment details) prevent a full 6. A score of 4 reflects strong contextualisation and largely reproducible methodology while acknowledging these gaps.

strengths: The conclusion restates key findings (quadratic trend, plateau/decline) and cites RÂ² to justify consistency with the analysis.
Model fits are compared and selection is justified based on statistical strength.
weaknesses: Extrapolation beyond the final pressure points is not sufficiently qualified.
Comparison to accepted scientific or published CoR values is only superficial.
Statistical support for the downturn region relies on limited data points without robust evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusion is coherent and aligned with the data (band 5 for relevance/support), its scientific context comparison is limited (band 3). Balancing these strands yields a mid-band score of 3.

strengths: Methodological weaknesses are identified specifically (gauge resolution, release mechanism, surface variability) with quantified impact where possible.
Relevant improvements are proposed (digital gauge, trigger-release, additional trials) with explanations of how they reduce errors.
weaknesses: Recommendations are not prioritised by feasibility or impact.
Some qualitative labels (e.g. âHighâ, âModerateâ) lack full quantitative justification.
Captioning of evaluation table could more clearly state its evaluative role.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation demonstrates clear explanation of specific weaknesses and realistic improvements, meriting a top-band placement. Minor issues in prioritisation and quantitative backing justify awarding 4 rather than the maximum.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student sets out a focused research question â âHow does the internal pressure of a basketball affect its coefficient of restitution during rebound?â â and embeds it in a very specific sporting and physical context (basketballs of size 7, rebound behaviour, game performance).  Background theory, definitions and practical motivations are supplied, so any reader unfamiliar with the sport immediately understands why pressure matters and why CoR is the suitable construct to study.  This level of contextualisation matches the descriptor of a âspecific and appropriate context,â warranting the top band; a minor lack of reference to official sport-regulation pressure ranges prevents awarding the full 6 marks.

Uncertainties are propagated for pressure, rebound height, average height and CoR; error bars use these values and are discussed in the text.  The parallax calculation and frame-rate analysis demonstrate appropriate depth.  Minor gaps (no uncertainty attached to quadratic maxima, no discussion of combined random/systematic error for pressure) keep the strand from a perfect 6, but overall the consideration is appropriate.

Controlled and independent variables are clearly identified, rationales are given, and methods for maintaining constants (surface type, temperature, drop height) are described.  The student explains why analogue resolution limits dominate uncertainty and justifies omitting parallax.  These explanations show understanding of how sufficiency and relevance of data support answering the question, satisfying the âexplainedâ standard, though the choice of only three trials keeps the mark to 5 rather than 6.

The evaluation table identifies specific weaknessesâpressure gauge resolution, manual release, surface variabilityâand for each explains the relative magnitude of their effect (e.g., 7.4 % energy loss to drag, negligible parallax).  This explicit discussion of impact satisfies the upper-band descriptor; minor qualitative labels ("High", "Moderate") without quantitative backing for all entries restrain the score to 5.

Step-by-step instructions, a labelled diagram, list of apparatus with uncertainties, and specification of seven discrete pressure settings and three video-recorded trials per setting allow another investigator to replicate the work with little ambiguity.  Only minor omissions (e.g., exact camera model/FOV, lighting conditions) remain, so reproduction is feasible; this aligns with the top-band requirement.  A single mark is withheld because a precise description of how the ball is aligned horizontally prior to release is missing.

Averages, CoR values, propagated uncertainties and two different regression models are carried out correctly, with correct formula use and appropriate software support.  Numerical consistency checks (significant figures, rounding) are mostly sound, though one or two mis-typed decimal separators appear.  Processing therefore satisfies the âappropriately and accuratelyâ criterion with only minor slips, meriting 5 marks in the top band.

For every major weakness the student offers a practical, relevant remedy (digital gauge with Â±0.1 psi, trigger-based drop, more trials).  Rationales indicate how each improvement would reduce systematic or random error, meeting the âexplainedâ standard.  Not all improvements are cost-balanced or prioritised, hence 5 rather than 6 marks.

Raw-data and processed tables are neat, labelled and include uncertainties.  Graphs have error bars on both axes, best-fit equations and RÂ² values.  Units and significant figures are generally consistent, but the formatting of some column headings is cluttered, so full precision and clarity are slightly compromised.  Nonetheless both clarity and precision are met overall, justifying band 5-6 with 5 marks.

The student briefly connects results to theoretical ideas (elastic deformation, energy loss, structural limits) and cites an engineering paper, but stops short of quantitatively comparing obtained CoR values to published standards or ideal gas expectations.  The comparison is therefore âsome relevantâ but not fully justified, appropriate for band 3-4; mid-band 3 marks awarded.

The conclusion restates the observed quadratic relationship, cites the 0.995 RÂ² value and identifies a plateau/decline above â13 psi, explicitly linking these findings to the hypothesis.  It remains fully consistent with the processed data and graphs, satisfying the highest-band descriptor; a single mark is dropped because statistical evidence for the downturn rests on extrapolation from only the final two pressure points.

annotations.comment: The introduction situates the research question within a clear sporting context and explains why CoR and pressure are linked, aiding reader understanding.
The discussion correctly highlights the plateau and downturn in rebound height, effectively interpreting the quadratic fit.
When introducing the CoR scatter graph, explicitly restate axis units and uncertainties for reader clarity.
Conclusion text effectively restates key findings with RÂ² values and threshold, supporting the hypothesis.
Be cautious extrapolating the downturn beyond the final data point; highlight that statistical support for that region is limited.
The conclusion clearly distinguishes model fits and justifies selection with high RÂ², reinforcing consistency with analysis.
The evaluation table systematically links error sources to impacts and improvements, demonstrating thorough selfâassessment.
Consider citing official basketball regulation pressure ranges (e.g. 7.5â8.5 psi) to strengthen context and justify chosen pressure settings.
The research question is concise and embedded in a specific context, clearly guiding the experimental focus.
The hypothesis is logically linked to theoretical background and prior studies, forecasting both increase and plateau in CoR.
The subscripts in the restitution equation are potentially confusing; clarify which velocities belong to the ball and surface consistently.
Deriving CoR via energy and kinematics is well chosen to minimize error in velocity measurements.
The description of the dependent variable calculation omits discussion of video resolution and its effect on height measurement precision.
While drop height control is detailed, specify how the release mechanism ensures the ball is consistently horizontal and without initial spin.
The experimental setup diagram is clear and supports reproducibility by illustrating key distances and apparatus placement.
Include the phoneâs camera model and field-of-view specifications to further reduce ambiguity in parallax error mitigation.
Raw data table headings are cluttered; simplify labels and align significant figures to improve clarity.
The averaging formula correctly condenses three trials into a single mean value, facilitating easier subsequent analysis.
The parallax angle calculation is thorough; consider limiting results to two decimal places for consistency with other uncertainties.
The stated pressure uncertainty contradicts the gauge increment given earlier; verify the analogue resolution and recalculate if needed.
Neglecting parallax error is justified given its magnitude is below tape resolution, which demonstrates appropriate uncertainty consideration.
Thereâs a typographical error â2.9.81â in the video uncertainty formula; correct this to ensure mathematical accuracy.
Combining rebound height uncertainties via square root sum is correctly applied, reflecting sound data processing.
The propagation formula for CoR uncertainty is valid; briefly discuss omission of atmospheric uncertainty to complete the analysis.
The processed data table is wellâorganised with uncertainties and supporting values, aiding clear interpretation.
How might the non-zero intercept in the pressure vs rebound graph be interpreted physically or corrected in modeling?
Improvement suggestions are relevant but would benefit from prioritisation by impact and feasibility to guide future work.
Caption would more clearly reflect its evaluative role by explicitly stating âEvaluation of methodological strengths and weaknesses.â

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
